# Jacobi Eigenvalue Algorithm


Iterative algorithm for symmetric matrices using plane rotations to zero off-diagonal entries.

## Givens Rotations


::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Definition: Givens Rotation
$G(p, q, \theta)$ is an identity matrix with a $2 \times 2$ rotation block at $(p, p), (p, q), (q, p), (q, q)$.

- **Similarity Transform:**  $\bA' = G^T \bA G$.
    
- **Effect:**  Modifies only rows/columns $p$ and $q$. Preserves eigenvalues ($\bA'$ is orthogonally similar to $\bA$).

:::


::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Theorem: Optimal Rotation Angle
To set $A'_{pq} = 0$, let $\tau = (A_{qq} - A_{pp}) / (2A_{pq})$. The optimal tangent $t = \tan\theta$ is:
$$t = \frac{\text{sign}(\tau)}{|\tau| + \sqrt{1 + \tau^2}}, \quad c = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+t^2}}, \quad s = ct.$$
Choosing the smaller root $|t| \leq 1$ ensures numerical stability and minimal perturbation.
:::


## Convergence and Cost


::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Theorem: Monotone Convergence
Let $\sigma^2_{\text{off}}(\bA) = \sum_{p < q} A_{pq}^2$. Each rotation reduces this norm:
$$\sigma^2_{\text{off}}(\bA') = \sigma^2_{\text{off}}(\bA) - A_{pq}^2.$$
**Convergence:**  Off-diagonals decrease monotonically. Convergence is ultimately **quadratic** (errors square each sweep).
:::


::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
**Performance vs.\ Accuracy:** 

- **Cost:**  $O(n^3)$ per sweep. Typically 5--15 sweeps for convergence. More expensive than QR.
    
- **Accuracy:**  Unlike QR, Jacobi achieves **high relative accuracy** on small eigenvalues.

:::


::: {.callout-warning icon=false}
## Exercise

1. Apply one Jacobi rotation to zero $A_{12}$ in $\bA = \begin{pmatrix}2 & 1 \\ 1 & 3\end{pmatrix}$.
    
1. Show that after zeroing $A_{pq}$, the new diagonal entries $A'_{pp}$ and $A'_{qq}$ are the eigenvalues of the $2 \times 2$ submatrix at $\{p, q\}$.
    
1. Perform one full cyclic sweep on $\bA = \begin{pmatrix}1&1&0\\1&2&1\\0&1&3\end{pmatrix}$ by hand.

:::
